sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.45 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: A$t$-$(v,k, )$design\/${ D}=(X,{ B})$contains a set$X$of$v$points and a set${ B}$of$k$-element subsets of~$X$called blocks\/ such that each$t$-element subset of~$X$is contained in$$blocks. Prove that there are no nontrivial block-transitive 6-designs. (We have shown that there are no nontrivial block-transitive 8-designs and there are certainly some block-transitive, even flag-transitive, 5-design...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
